the_deck_body: Scopa uses a 40-card Italian deck: four suits (Coins ♦, Cups ♥, Swords ♠, Clubs ♣), ranks 1–7 plus J (8), Q (9), K (10).
goal_body: Score more points than your opponent across one or more hands. A game ends when a player reaches the target score (typically 11 for 2 players).
setup_body: Shuffle the deck. Deal 3 cards to each player, then place 4 cards face-up on the table. If the initial table contains 3 or more Kings, re-deal.
playing_select: Select a card from your hand and play it.
playing_capture: <0>Capture:</0> if your card's rank matches one or more table cards (or a subset summing to your card's rank), take them. If multiple captures exist, choose which to take. If a single-card match exists, you must prefer it over multi-card combinations.
playing_discard: <0>Discard:</0> if no capture is possible, your card stays on the table.
playing_redeal: When all hands are empty, deal 3 more cards each until the deck runs out.
scopa_body: If you capture all cards from the table, that's a <0>scopa</0> — worth 1 bonus point. Exception: the last capture of the hand is never a scopa.
end_body: When the deck is exhausted and all hands are empty, any cards remaining on the table go to the last player who captured. Then scores are calculated.
scoring_scope: <0>Scope</0> — 1 point per scopa swept during play.
scoring_carte: <0>Carte</0> — 1 point to whoever captured the most cards.
scoring_ori: <0>Ori (Coins)</0> — 1 point to whoever captured the most Coins cards.
scoring_settebello: <0>Settebello</0> — 1 point to whoever captured the 7 of Coins.
scoring_primiera: <0>Primiera</0> — 1 point to whoever has the highest primiera score. Each suit contributes its best card: 7=21, 6=18, Ace=16, 5=15, 4=14, 3=13, 2=12, J/Q/K=10. A player must hold at least one card of each suit to score primiera.
variant_re_bello: <0>Re Bello</0> — 10♦ (King of Coins) scores 1 extra point.
variant_settanta: <0>Settanta</0> — 1 extra point to whoever holds all four 7s.
variant_napola: <0>Napola</0> — holding Ace+2+3 of Coins scores 3 points; each additional Coin card in sequence adds 1 more.
variant_scopa_d_assi: <0>Scopa d'Assi</0> — playing an Ace captures the entire table.
variant_inversa: <0>Inversa</0> — reversed game: lowest score wins. Avoid capturing valuable cards.
variant_quindici: <0>Quindici</0> — instead of matching ranks, your card and the captured cards must sum to exactly 15.
variant_undici: <0>Undici</0> — same as Quindici but cards must sum to 11.
variant_veneto: <0>Veneto primiera</0> — uses slightly different card values for primiera.
variant_milano: <0>Milano primiera</0> — whoever holds the most 7s wins; ties broken by 6s, then Aces.
